hypothesis: If chemical foundation models pre-trained on large SMILES corpora are used to generate transferable embeddings that are aggregated for formulations and combined with device variables, then prediction accuracy, out-of-distribution robustness, and alignment of moiety–property correlations for battery-relevant properties from molecules to devices will exceed those of traditional fingerprints and non-foundational baselines because pretraining captures chemically meaningful features that generalize across scales.
